property.name: java.library.path
property.value: C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21\bin;C:\windows\Sun\Java\bin;C:\windows\system32;C:\windows;C:/Users/manis/.gemini/antigravity-ide/bin;C:\windows\system32;C:\windows;C:\windows\System32\Wbem;C:\windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\windows\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21\bin;C:\Program Files\apache-maven-3.9.11\bin;C:\Program Files\Git\cmd;C:\Program Files\Redis\;C:\Program Files\Docker\Docker\resources\bin;C:\Program Files\nodejs\;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21\bin;C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA Community Edition 2025.2.3\bin;C:\Users\manis\AppData\Roaming\npm;C:\Users\manis\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Users\manis\AppData\Local\Programs\Antigravity\bin;C:\Users\manis\AppData\Local\Programs\Antigravity IDE\bin;.

testcase.name: testCancelShipmentSuccess
testcase.system-out: 2026-07-11T11:12:22.551+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryShipmentService : Cancelling shipment for order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542, Shipment ID: SHIP123 2026-07-11T11:12:22.569+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryShipmentService : Shipment cancelled successfully for order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542

testcase.name: testWebhookProcessing
testcase.system-out: 2026-07-11T11:12:22.575+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryWebhookService : Received iCarry Webhook. AWB: AWB123, Status code: 21 2026-07-11T11:12:22.576+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryWebhookService : Updating order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542 via Webhook. ShipmentStatus -> DELIVERED, OrderStatus -> DELIVERED

testcase.name: testTrackShipment

error.message: Tracking API failed: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'post' method: client.post( "/api_track_shipment", {"awb" = [AWB123]}, true ); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.IcarryTrackingService.trackShipment(IcarryTrackingService.java:44) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. client.post(null, null, false); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.ShippingServicesTest.testTrackShipment(ShippingServicesTest.java:170) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class.

testcase.error: com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.exception.IcarryApiException: Tracking API failed: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'post' method: client.post( "/api_track_shipment", {"awb" = [AWB123]}, true ); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.IcarryTrackingService.trackShipment(IcarryTrackingService.java:44) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. client.post(null, null, false); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.ShippingServicesTest.testTrackShipment(ShippingServicesTest.java:170) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). Please check: - this invocation of 'post' method: client.post( "/api_track_shipment", {"awb" = [AWB123]}, true ); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.IcarryTrackingService.trackShipment(IcarryTrackingService.java:44) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. client.post(null, null, false); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.ShippingServicesTest.testTrackShipment(ShippingServicesTest.java:170) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class. at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.client.IcarryClient.post(IcarryClient.java:43) at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.IcarryTrackingService.trackShipment(IcarryTrackingService.java:44) ... 4 more

testcase.name: testGenerateLabel
testcase.system-out: 2026-07-11T11:12:22.605+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryLabelService : Generating shipping label for order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542, Shipment ID: SHIP123

testcase.name: testGetEstimate
testcase.system-out: 2026-07-11T11:12:22.609+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryEstimateService : Requesting shipment estimate from: 110001 to: 400001 for weight: 500g

testcase.name: testBookShipmentDuplicate
testcase.system-out: 2026-07-11T11:12:22.616+05:30 WARN 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryShipmentService : Shipment already booked for order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542. Shipment ID: SHIP_EXISTING

testcase.name: testPickupScheduling
testcase.system-out: 2026-07-11T11:12:22.622+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryPickupService : Requesting package pickup slot for order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542, Shipment ID: SHIP123 2026-07-11T11:12:22.623+05:30 INFO 1220 --- [ main] c.l.e.s.service.IcarryPickupService : Pickup successfully scheduled for order ID: 23db3d7a-683b-372b-8036-95da3ae5c542

testcase.name: testBookShipmentSuccess

error.message: Booking failed: Strict stubbing argument mismatch. Please check: - this invocation of 'post' method: client.post( "/api_add_shipment_surface", {"consignee[name]" = [John Doe], "consignee[mobile]" = [9876543210], "consignee[address]" = [123 Street ], "consignee[city]" = [New Delhi], "consignee[pincode]" = [110001], "consignee[state]" = [DL], "consignee[country_code]" = [IN], "parcel[type]" = [P], "parcel[value]" = [200.0], "parcel[contents]" = [Item A x2], "weight" = [500], "weight_unit" = [gm], "length" = [10], "breadth" = [10], "height" = [10], "pickup_address_id" = [null]}, false ); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.IcarryShipmentService.bookShipmentForOrder(IcarryShipmentService.java:101) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. client.post(null, null, false); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.ShippingServicesTest.testBookShipmentSuccess(ShippingServicesTest.java:115) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class.

Please check: - this invocation of 'post' method: client.post( "/api_add_shipment_surface", {"consignee[name]" = [John Doe], "consignee[mobile]" = [9876543210], "consignee[address]" = [123 Street ], "consignee[city]" = [New Delhi], "consignee[pincode]" = [110001], "consignee[state]" = [DL], "consignee[country_code]" = [IN], "parcel[type]" = [P], "parcel[value]" = [200.0], "parcel[contents]" = [Item A x2], "weight" = [500], "weight_unit" = [gm], "length" = [10], "breadth" = [10], "height" = [10], "pickup_address_id" = [null]}, false ); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.IcarryShipmentService.bookShipmentForOrder(IcarryShipmentService.java:101) - has following stubbing(s) with different arguments: 1. client.post(null, null, false); -> at com.lemonacademy.ecommerce.shipping.service.ShippingServicesTest.testBookShipmentSuccess(ShippingServicesTest.java:115) Typically, stubbing argument mismatch indicates user mistake when writing tests. Mockito fails early so that you can debug potential problem easily. However, there are legit scenarios when this exception generates false negative signal: - stubbing the same method multiple times using 'given().will()' or 'when().then()' API Please use 'will().given()' or 'doReturn().when()' API for stubbing. - stubbed method is intentionally invoked with different arguments by code under test Please use default or 'silent' JUnit Rule (equivalent of Strictness.LENIENT). For more information see javadoc for PotentialStubbingProblem class.
